title: Preserving America's Game
summary: Executive Order 14396, issued on March 20, 2026, aims to preserve the Army-Navy Game, known as "America's Game," by preventing scheduling conflicts with the College Football Playoffs (CFP) and other postseason games. The order establishes a policy that no college football game should be broadcast in a manner that directly conflicts with the Army-Navy Game, traditionally held on the second Saturday in December. The Secretary of Commerce and the FCC Chairman are tasked with coordinating with relevant organizations and media partners to establish an exclusive broadcast window for the Army-Navy Game. The FCC Chairman will also review broadcast licensees' public interest obligations to ensure the game remains a national service event. This order is intended to maintain the national focus on Military Service Academies and support this vital morale-building event.
